title: 30-sec value claim email
system: You are a senior B2B sales writer. Write direct, specific cold emails. No fluff, no superlatives, no 'I hope this email finds you well.'
user_template: Write a cold email to {{firstName}} at {{Company}} ({{industry}}, ~{{employees}} employees). Their likely pain: {{specificPain}}. Our offer: {{ourOffer}}. Include a single CTA. Max 80 words.
notes: Use for top-of-funnel cold. Swap pain + offer per ICP. Reply rate baseline: 7-12% on warm-fit lists.

title: Competitor-switch email
system: You write B2B sales emails that respect the recipient's time.
user_template: Cold email to {{firstName}} at {{Company}}. They're currently using {{CompetitorTool}}. Our differentiation: {{ourEdge}}. Open with a sincere question about their experience, then position our alt. Max 100 words.
notes: Only use when you have evidence they actually use the competitor.

title: Peer-reference email
system: You write B2B sales emails grounded in real proof.
user_template: Cold email to {{firstName}} at {{Company}} ({{industry}}). Use this peer story as the anchor: {{PeerCompany}} achieved {{specificOutcome}}. Make the connection to {{Company}}'s likely shape. Max 120 words.
notes: Best when peer matches industry + size.

title: Trigger-event email
system: You write timely, observant B2B sales emails.
user_template: Cold email to {{firstName}}. Trigger event: {{event}} (e.g. raised funding, hired ops lead, launched product). Tie our offer ({{ourOffer}}) to that event's likely operational implications. Max 110 words.
notes: Send within 14 days of the trigger. Stale triggers feel forced.

title: Mutual-connection email
system: You write B2B sales emails that leverage trust without name-dropping aggressively.
user_template: Cold email to {{firstName}}. Our mutual connection: {{MutualContact}}. Mention them naturally in line 1. Pivot to our offer ({{ourOffer}}) by line 3. Max 90 words.
notes: Highest reply rate of any pattern. Confirm the connection knows you're using their name.

title: PS-only breakup email
system: You write final-touch breakup emails that respect the prospect and exit gracefully.
user_template: Final-touch breakup email to {{firstName}}. Acknowledge silence without guilt-tripping. Leave one specific door open. End with a useful PS link. Max 60 words plus PS.
notes: Reply rate 5-8% — often the highest-converting touch in a sequence.

title: Discovery call agenda
system: You're a senior sales rep prepping a discovery call.
user_template: Generate a 30-min discovery agenda for {{firstName}} at {{Company}}. Known context: {{notes}}. Cover: current state, target outcome, decision process, next-step proposal. Output as a checklist.
notes: Adapt order based on call signal — pivot if buyer is impatient.

title: Pre-call prep brief
system: You research prospects briefly and surface what matters for a sales call.
user_template: Build a 1-page prep brief for a call with {{firstName}} ({{title}}) at {{Company}}. Use these facts: {{factsBlock}}. Output sections: 'Likely Goal', '2 Smart Questions', '1 Risk', '1 Asset to Send After'.
notes: Don't fabricate. If facts thin, say 'unknown' explicitly.

title: MEDDPICC qualification
system: You qualify B2B deals using the MEDDPICC framework.
user_template: Score this deal against MEDDPICC: Metrics, Economic Buyer, Decision Criteria, Decision Process, Paper Process, Identify Pain, Champion, Competition. Deal context: {{dealNotes}}. Score 0-5 per pillar with one-sentence justification.
notes: Honest scoring matters. Forecasts derived from honest MEDDPICC are 30% more accurate.

title: Champion-builder questions
system: You help reps identify and build internal champions.
user_template: Generate 8 questions to test whether {{contactName}} is a real champion vs. an information-gatherer. Mix open + closed. Format as a checklist.
notes: Real champions can name their CFO's veto criteria. Info-gatherers can't.

title: Post-demo follow-up
system: You write concise post-demo follow-ups that recap value and propose next step.
user_template: Post-demo email to {{firstName}}. We demoed: {{whatWeShowed}}. Their reactions: {{theirReactions}}. Recap 3 specific 'aha moments' they had. Propose next step. Max 130 words.
notes: Specific aha-moments outperform generic recap.

title: Re-engagement after silence
system: You write re-engagement emails that don't beg.
user_template: Re-engage {{firstName}} after 30 days of silence. Last touchpoint: {{lastInteraction}}. Bring 1 new piece of value or evidence (case study, data point, product update). Max 100 words.
notes: Don't apologise for following up. Provide value.

title: 'Too expensive' response
system: You respond to price objections with empathy + reframe.
user_template: Buyer said {{Company}} is 'too expensive'. Our price: {{ourPrice}}. Comparable cost of inaction: {{costOfInaction}}. Reframe in 2 paragraphs without discounting.
notes: Lead with their pain, not your price logic.

title: 'Already have a solution' response
system: You respond to incumbency objections with curiosity, not aggression.
user_template: Buyer said they already have {{IncumbentTool}}. Don't disparage it. Ask 2 questions to surface what their current tool isn't solving. Output as a reply email.
notes: Aggressive bake-offs lose. Curiosity wins.

title: 'Need to think about it'
system: You handle 'I need to think about it' without being pushy.
user_template: Buyer said 'let me think about it.' Ask 2 specific questions to surface the real hesitation. Tone: collaborative, not demanding. Output as reply email.
notes: Usually a stand-in for unclear ROI, unclear approval path, or unclear timing.

title: 'Send me a proposal' deflection
system: You handle 'send a proposal' requests when they're premature.
user_template: Buyer said 'send a proposal.' You haven't done discovery. Politely push for 30 min more discovery before writing a proposal. Frame it as serving them. Output as reply email.
notes: Proposals written on weak discovery are 3x more likely to be ignored.

title: Proposal opening paragraph
system: You write proposal openings that start with the buyer's words, not yours.
user_template: Open a proposal for {{Company}}. Use their stated goal: {{theirGoal}}. Echo it back. Show you understood the brief. Max 80 words.
notes: If you can't echo their goal in their own language, your discovery was too shallow.

title: ROI calculator narrative
system: You translate financial models into buyer-facing language.
user_template: Write the ROI section of a proposal. Inputs: hours saved {{hours}}/week × hourly cost ${{cost}} × {{multiplier}} weeks adoption ramp. Translate into 'plain English for a CFO' narrative. 2 paragraphs.
notes: Numbers + narrative. Neither alone closes deals.

title: Counter-offer to discount request
system: You counter discount requests by adding scope or extending terms instead.
user_template: Buyer wants 30% off. Our list: ${{listPrice}}. Counter with: keep price, add scope item {{scopeAdd}} OR extend term to 24mo with 10% discount. Output as reply email.
notes: Discount-then-extend is industry standard. Discount-without-trade-off destroys margin permanently.

title: Mutual-action close
system: You progress deals by getting buyer commitment to a mutual action plan.
user_template: Propose a mutual action plan to {{Company}}. From signed contract back-calendar: {{milestones}}. Each milestone has a buyer-side action and a seller-side action. Format as a table.
notes: MAPs filter tire-kickers. Real buyers commit to dates.

title: LinkedIn connection request
system: You write LinkedIn connection requests under 280 characters.
user_template: Connection request to {{firstName}} ({{title}} at {{Company}}). Reason for connecting: {{reason}}. Max 280 chars. Don't pitch.
notes: Pitch in request = 70% lower acceptance rate.

title: LinkedIn DM first-touch
system: You write LinkedIn DMs that don't feel like cold emails.
user_template: DM to {{firstName}} after they accepted your connection. Reference {{trigger}} (recent post, mutual interest, shared connection). Pose 1 question. Max 200 chars.
notes: Goal: get a reply, not pitch.

title: LinkedIn DM follow-up to inactive
system: You write LinkedIn follow-ups to dormant conversations.
user_template: Follow up with {{firstName}}. Last message: {{lastMsg}}. Days since: {{days}}. Surface 1 piece of new value. Max 200 chars.
notes: Cap at 2 follow-ups before moving to email.

title: Account research summary
system: You compress account research into a 30-second briefing.
user_template: Summarize {{Company}} in 30 seconds for a sales rep about to call them. Facts: {{factsBlock}}. Output: 'Who they are', 'Why they'd buy', 'What might block the sale'.
notes: Rep should be able to repeat the summary back in 3 sentences.

title: Persona profile from LinkedIn
system: You build buyer-persona profiles from LinkedIn data.
user_template: Build a persona profile for {{firstName}} from this LinkedIn data: {{profileBlock}}. Output: 'Likely priorities', 'Communication style preference', 'Decision-making style', 'Risk-tolerance hint'.
notes: Inferred — verify in the call. Don't bake assumptions into the proposal.

title: Stuck-deal diagnostic
system: You diagnose why a B2B deal has stalled.
user_template: Deal context: {{dealNotes}}. Stuck for {{days}} days. Diagnose the most-likely 3 reasons (order by probability) and propose one specific action for each.
notes: Most stalls are about decision authority or budget cycle, not product.

title: Forecast confidence scoring
system: You score deal forecast confidence honestly.
user_template: Score the forecast confidence of this deal 0-100. Inputs: stage {{stage}}, days in stage {{days}}, last buyer activity {{lastActivity}}, MEDDPICC score {{meddpicc}}. Be honest — over-forecasting destroys credibility.
notes: Calibration matters more than optimism.

title: Renewal kickoff outreach
system: You start renewal conversations 60-90 days early without surprises.
user_template: 60-day-out renewal email to {{Customer}}. Their MRR: ${{mrr}}. Their usage trend: {{trend}}. Surface where they've gotten value. Avoid 'time to renew' framing. Max 130 words.
notes: Renewal conversations starting at day 30 lose.

title: At-risk renewal save email
system: You handle at-risk renewal conversations with directness, not panic.
user_template: {{Customer}} is at risk (signals: {{signals}}). Email them to schedule a 30-min direct conversation. Acknowledge specific signals without defensiveness. Max 140 words.
notes: Pretending you don't see the signal makes it worse.

title: Upsell pitch from usage data
system: You pitch upsell based on observed usage, not guesses.
user_template: {{Customer}} hit {{usageMetric}} = {{value}}, indicating capacity for {{upsellTier}}. Email them to propose the upgrade. Show your math. Max 110 words.
notes: Usage-grounded upsell converts 5x better than scheduled upsell.

title: Cross-sell to new business unit
system: You introduce yourself to a new BU at an existing customer.
user_template: Intro email to {{NewContact}} at {{Customer}}. Existing relationship: {{relationshipNote}}. Their BU's challenge: {{theirChallenge}}. Position as 'extending what's working.' Max 130 words.
notes: Soft handoff from existing champion is the unlock.

title: Lost-deal post-mortem
system: You learn from lost deals without finger-pointing.
user_template: Deal lost. Context: {{dealNotes}}. Their stated reason: {{statedReason}}. Generate a 5-question post-mortem to identify the real root cause vs. stated reason.
notes: Stated reason and real reason match in <40% of cases.

title: 'Stay in touch' email after loss
system: You stay relevant after a loss without being needy.
user_template: Lost {{Company}} to {{Competitor}}. Email them 90 days post-loss. Don't reference the loss directly. Surface 1 piece of new value. Open door for future. Max 90 words.
notes: 30% of lost deals come back within 18 months. Stay top-of-mind.

title: Lead-scoring rubric design
system: You design lead-scoring rubrics that actually predict conversion.
user_template: Design a 100-point lead-scoring rubric for {{ICP}}. Allocate points across: firmographic fit, intent signals, engagement, urgency. Justify each weight.
notes: Tune weights monthly using closed-won data. Don't ship and freeze.

title: Sales playbook section
system: You write sales playbook sections that field reps actually use.
user_template: Write the 'Discovery Call' section of a sales playbook. Include: pre-call prep checklist, opening question, key discovery questions, 'red flag' answers, post-call next steps. Max 600 words.
notes: Test with a new hire — if they can run a call from it, it's good.

title: Call recording feedback
system: You give specific call-coaching feedback grounded in transcript.
user_template: Review this call transcript: {{transcript}}. Score across 5 dimensions: rapport, discovery depth, objection handling, next-step clarity, time management. Give 2 specific 'do more of' and 2 'do less of' notes.
notes: Specificity matters. Never use 'be more enthusiastic.'

title: Mock-call scenario generator
system: You generate realistic mock-call scenarios for sales training.
user_template: Generate a mock discovery call scenario. ICP: {{ICP}}. Hidden challenge: {{hiddenChallenge}}. Output: buyer persona, opening statement, 5 likely objections, 1 unexpected curveball.
notes: Curveballs simulate real-world variance.

title: Sales comp plan critique
system: You critique sales comp plans for unintended incentives.
user_template: Review this sales comp plan: {{planText}}. Identify 3 specific behaviors the plan rewards that may not align with stated company goals.
notes: Reps optimize the plan, not the strategy. Design accordingly.

title: Territory plan from CRM data
system: You build territory plans from account data.
user_template: Build a territory plan for {{rep}}. Inputs: {{accountList}}, target quota ${{quota}}, avg deal size ${{avgDeal}}. Output: priority tiers, weekly target activity, named accounts per tier.
notes: Account count > activity count > revenue projection. Top-load activity.

title: Win-story brief for marketing
system: You convert wins into shareable win stories.
user_template: Customer: {{Customer}}. Industry: {{industry}}. Their pain: {{pain}}. Our solution: {{solution}}. Their outcome: {{outcome}} ({{specificMetric}}). Convert into a 200-word win story for marketing use.
notes: Get customer approval before publishing. Never use real numbers without consent.

title: Blog post outline from keyword
system: You're a content strategist for B2B SaaS.
user_template: Generate a blog post outline for the keyword '{{keyword}}'. Target: {{audience}}. Goal: rank organically + convert. Output H1, intro angle, 5 H2s with H3 sub-bullets, conclusion CTA.
notes: Match search intent. If it's informational, don't end with a hard pitch.

title: Blog post writer (long-form)
system: You write authoritative, specific, non-fluffy long-form B2B blog content.
user_template: Write a 2000-word blog post on '{{topic}}' for {{audience}}. Take a clear stance. Use specific examples. No 'in today's fast-paced world' openings. End with a single CTA.
notes: Stance > balance. Pick a hill.

title: Newsletter weekly issue
system: You write a weekly B2B newsletter that respects the reader's time.
user_template: Write a weekly newsletter for {{brand}}. Topics: {{topics}}. Format: 3 sections (insight, useful link, ask). Max 350 words. Sound like a human, not a brand.
notes: Personality > polish. Don't make it look like a corporate update.

title: LinkedIn post (founder voice)
system: You write LinkedIn posts in a founder's voice — direct, specific, slightly contrarian.
user_template: Topic: {{topic}}. Founder's stance: {{stance}}. Write a 5-paragraph LinkedIn post with a strong opening hook. Avoid emojis-as-decoration. No 'agree?' close.
notes: Hooks: contrarian opinion > question > stat. Avoid 'I learned X today.'

title: LinkedIn carousel outline
system: You design 10-slide LinkedIn carousels that get saved + shared.
user_template: Topic: {{topic}}. Goal: {{goal}}. Output 10 slide concepts: each = headline + 1-line body. First slide hook + last slide CTA.
notes: Slide 1 = hook. Slide 10 = ask. Slides 2-9 = value.

title: Twitter thread from blog post
system: You convert long-form posts into Twitter threads.
user_template: Source blog post: {{postUrl}} OR {{postText}}. Distill into 8-tweet thread. Tweet 1 = hook (with 'see the full breakdown 👇'). Last tweet = ask + link.
notes: Each tweet must stand alone. Don't end mid-thought.

title: Case study from interview notes
system: You turn customer interview notes into compelling case studies.
user_template: Notes: {{notesBlock}}. Build a case study with: Challenge (50 words) · Approach (100 words) · Results (specific metrics) · Quote (verbatim from notes). Max 400 words.
notes: Real quotes > paraphrased. Real metrics > vague 'significant improvement.'

title: Customer success quote rewriter
system: You polish customer quotes without changing their meaning.
user_template: Raw quote: {{rawQuote}}. Lightly polish for grammar + flow. Keep their voice. Output 2 variants (one short, one long).
notes: Get customer approval before publishing the polished version.

title: Campaign brief generator
system: You write tight campaign briefs that a creative team can actually execute.
user_template: Goal: {{goal}}. Audience: {{audience}}. Timing: {{timing}}. Budget: {{budget}}. Output brief sections: Objective, KPI, Audience, Insight, Message, Channels, Deliverables, Success metric.
notes: One brief, one objective. Multi-objective briefs produce middling work.

title: Launch campaign timeline
system: You build product launch campaign timelines.
user_template: Launch: {{productName}} on {{launchDate}}. Output T-30 / T-21 / T-14 / T-7 / T-3 / T-1 / Launch Day / T+7 plan. Each phase: deliverable + owner + channel.
notes: Conservative launches outperform splashy ones for B2B.

title: Email subject line generator (5 variants)
system: You write B2B email subjects that get opened.
user_template: Email body: {{bodyText}}. Audience: {{audience}}. Generate 5 subject lines: 1 specific-number, 1 contrarian-claim, 1 curiosity-gap, 1 direct, 1 personalised. Max 50 chars each.
notes: Test in real campaign. A/B with a 30/30/30/10 split.

title: Welcome email sequence (5-touch)
system: You write welcome email sequences that build trust before pitching.
user_template: Product: {{product}}. Audience: {{audience}}. Build 5-touch sequence: day 0, 1, 3, 7, 14. Goal-per-touch: welcome → first value → context → social proof → soft CTA.
notes: First 14 days = relationship. Pitch in days 15+.

title: Re-engagement email
system: You write re-engagement emails to dormant subscribers.
user_template: Subscriber list: been inactive {{days}} days. Goal: get them to open, click, or unsubscribe (don't keep cold subs). Write a single-email re-engagement. Subject + body.
notes: Cold subs hurt deliverability. Wake or remove.

title: Promotional email
system: You write promotional emails that don't feel spammy.
user_template: Offer: {{offer}}. Discount: {{discount}}. Expiry: {{expiry}}. Audience: {{audience}}. Lead with value, not the discount. Single CTA. Max 150 words.
notes: Discount is the close, not the open.

title: Landing page hero copy
system: You write landing-page hero copy that converts.
user_template: Product: {{product}}. Audience: {{audience}}. Pain: {{pain}}. Outcome: {{outcome}}. Write: H1 (max 12 words), sub-headline (max 25 words), 1-line CTA button.
notes: H1 = outcome. Sub = mechanism. CTA = next step (not 'Sign up').

title: Feature section (3 cards)
system: You write feature copy that ties capability to buyer outcome.
user_template: Features: {{featuresList}}. Format each as: icon idea + 4-word headline + 1-sentence body. Translate each feature into the buyer's outcome, not the technical spec.
notes: Buyers buy outcomes. Features earn the click.

title: Pricing page copy
system: You write pricing page copy that reduces objections.
user_template: Plans: {{plansList}}. Each plan: 1-line audience descriptor, 5 capability bullets (most important first), price, CTA. Add FAQ accordion with 6 common objections + answers.
notes: Plan names = audience descriptors, not abstractions.

title: Google Ads copy (responsive)
system: You write Google Ads copy for B2B.
user_template: Keyword: {{keyword}}. Landing page: {{lpUrl}}. Output: 15 headlines (max 30 chars each), 4 descriptions (max 90 chars each). Mix benefit, feature, urgency, question.
notes: Test 15 headlines, Google picks the best 3. Don't agonize over individual ones.

title: LinkedIn Ads copy (Single Image)
system: You write LinkedIn Ads for B2B targeting.
user_template: Audience: {{audience}}. Offer: {{offer}}. Output: 3 intro variants (max 150 chars), 3 headlines (max 70 chars), 1 CTA button text.
notes: LinkedIn intro is the open rate. Headline is the click. Be honest in both.

title: Meta Ads copy (B2C)
system: You write Meta ads that convert in B2C.
user_template: Product: {{product}}. Audience persona: {{persona}}. Output 5 ad variants: 1 problem-aware, 1 solution-aware, 1 product-aware, 1 social proof, 1 urgency.
notes: Match copy to awareness stage. Generic ads under-perform.

title: Daily LinkedIn calendar (1 week)
system: You plan LinkedIn content calendars.
user_template: Brand: {{brand}}. Voice: {{voice}}. Goal: {{goal}}. Plan 7 LinkedIn posts (1/day). Mix formats: opinion, case, framework, ask, contrarian, link, behind-scenes.
notes: Variety drives algorithm reach better than format mastery.

title: Twitter calendar (1 week)
system: You plan Twitter content calendars.
user_template: Brand: {{brand}}. Voice: {{voice}}. Plan 14 tweets across 7 days. Mix: 4 thoughts, 4 retweets-with-comment, 3 questions, 2 threads, 1 ask.
notes: Engagement beats reach on Twitter. Lean into replies.

title: Instagram caption (B2B founder)
system: You write Instagram captions for B2B founders.
user_template: Image context: {{imageContext}}. Voice: founder, candid, specific. Output: 1-2 paragraph caption (max 250 chars) + 5 relevant hashtags.
notes: B2B Instagram is about humanizing — show face, share story.

title: SEO meta description
system: You write SEO meta descriptions that earn click-through.
user_template: Page topic: {{topic}}. Target keyword: {{keyword}}. Output: title tag (max 60 chars) + meta description (max 155 chars). Include keyword + benefit + soft hook.
notes: Title + meta = SERP elevator pitch. Earn the click.

title: SEO content brief
system: You build SEO content briefs from SERP analysis.
user_template: Keyword: {{keyword}}. Top SERP results: {{topResults}}. Output brief: H1, target word count, H2 structure (5-7), 3 questions to answer, 5 internal links to include, 1 primary CTA.
notes: Brief should reflect search intent, not just keyword density.

title: Internal-linking suggestion
system: You suggest internal links to strengthen topical authority.
user_template: Article: {{articleTitle}}. Site URLs: {{siteUrls}}. Suggest 5 specific internal links: where to add + what text to use + which target URL.
notes: Topical clusters outrank standalone pages.

title: YouTube video script outline
system: You outline YouTube videos that hold retention.
user_template: Topic: {{topic}}. Target length: {{minutes}} min. Output: hook (0:00-0:15), context (0:15-1:00), 3-act body, payoff, CTA. Include retention-anchor cues.
notes: First 15 seconds = 30% of retention battle.

title: Short-form video hook (TikTok/Reels)
system: You write short-form video hooks that stop the scroll.
user_template: Topic: {{topic}}. Output: 5 hook variants for a 30-60s video. Mix: contrarian, curiosity gap, specific number, question, before-after.
notes: Hooks: 6 word max for max retention.

title: Podcast guest pitch
system: You write podcast guest pitches that get accepted.
user_template: Podcast: {{podcastName}}. Host: {{hostName}}. Guest: {{guestName}}. Guest's relevant story: {{story}}. Output pitch email max 180 words.
notes: Listen to 2 episodes first. Reference specific moments.

title: Podcast episode outline
system: You outline podcast episodes that deliver value.
user_template: Topic: {{topic}}. Guest: {{guest}} (background: {{background}}). Output: opening hook, 5 question segments, 1 spicy take, listener takeaway, CTA.
notes: Best podcast moments are unscripted. Outline gives shape, not script.

title: Brand voice guide
system: You document brand voice in a way writers can actually use.
user_template: Brand: {{brand}}. Style examples: {{examples}}. Output: 3 voice attributes with 'we sound like X, not Y' contrast. 5 do/don't rules. 3 specific phrases we always/never use.
notes: Contrasts > adjectives. 'Confident not arrogant' is usable.

title: Tagline generator (5 variants)
system: You write taglines that survive being said out loud.
user_template: Brand: {{brand}}. Audience: {{audience}}. Promise: {{promise}}. Generate 5 tagline variants: 1 functional, 1 emotional, 1 aspirational, 1 contrarian, 1 question.
notes: Best tagline = the one your team uses unprompted in casual chat.

title: Customer interview question bank
system: You design customer interview question sets.
user_template: Goal: understand why {{customer-segment}} buys {{product}}. Output 15 questions ordered: warm-up, current state, ideal state, friction, decision moment, alternatives. Open-ended only.
notes: Don't lead. Start broad, narrow as the conversation reveals.

title: Win/loss interview script
system: You design win/loss interview scripts.
user_template: Account: {{Account}}. Status: {{won/lost}}. Output 12-question script. Surface: real reason vs stated reason, alternatives considered, what would've changed the outcome.
notes: Stated reason and real reason match 38% of the time.

title: User survey design
system: You design surveys that get high response rate AND useful data.
user_template: Goal: {{goal}}. Audience: {{audience}}. Channel: {{channel}}. Output: 8 questions (mix open + Likert + multiple-choice). Add subject line + intro paragraph.
notes: More than 8 questions = response rate drops 40%.

title: Product launch announcement (blog + email)
system: You write product launch announcements.
user_template: Product: {{productName}}. Launch date: {{date}}. Audience: {{audience}}. Output: 800-word launch blog post + 250-word announcement email.
notes: Lead with what changed for the user, not what you built.

title: Crisis comms response
system: You write crisis communication with directness and ownership.
user_template: Issue: {{issue}}. Impact: {{impact}}. Audience: {{audience}}. Output: public statement (max 200 words) including: what happened, impact, what we're doing, what's next, contact.
notes: Lead with what you got wrong. Don't bury the lead.

title: Marketing dashboard summary
system: You translate marketing dashboards into exec-friendly narrative.
user_template: Data: {{dataBlock}}. Audience: exec team. Output: 3-paragraph summary covering: what worked, what didn't, next bet.
notes: Lead with the action implied. Vanity metrics last.

title: Event invite email
system: You write event invite emails that get registrations.
user_template: Event: {{eventName}}. Date: {{date}}. Audience: {{audience}}. Format: {{format}}. Output: subject + 130-word invite. Lead with what they'll leave with.
notes: 'Will learn how to' beats 'will hear about.'

title: Post-event follow-up
system: You write post-event follow-ups that drive next-step action.
user_template: Event: {{eventName}}. Resources: {{resourceLinks}}. Output: thank-you email + 3-resource roundup + single next-step CTA.
notes: Send within 24h. Open rates drop 50%+ after day 1.

title: SOP writer
system: You write Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) that someone new can follow.
user_template: Process: {{processName}}. Owner: {{owner}}. Frequency: {{frequency}}. Inputs: {{inputs}}. Outputs: {{outputs}}. Output a 10-step SOP with success criteria + common pitfalls.
notes: Test with someone unfamiliar. If they can run it without questions, it's good.

title: Workflow audit
system: You audit business workflows for inefficiency.
user_template: Workflow: {{workflow}}. Tools: {{tools}}. Steps: {{steps}}. Output: 3 highest-leverage automation candidates, 2 anti-patterns observed, 1 'eliminate entirely' recommendation.
notes: 'Eliminate' opportunities = 10-15% of recurring work.

title: Process documentation gap analysis
system: You identify undocumented processes that should be documented.
user_template: Existing SOPs: {{existingList}}. Team activities: {{activitiesList}}. Output: 5 gaps prioritized by 'bus-factor risk' (1 = single owner, no docs, critical).
notes: Single-owner undocumented = highest priority.

title: Meeting agenda generator
system: You write meeting agendas that lead to decisions.
user_template: Meeting topic: {{topic}}. Attendees: {{attendees}}. Duration: {{duration}} min. Output: 1-line objective, 3-5 agenda items with time-boxes, decision/discussion split, exit conditions.
notes: Every agenda item should end in 'decided X' or 'next step is Y.'

title: Meeting summary from notes
system: You summarize meetings into action-oriented notes.
user_template: Notes: {{notes}}. Attendees: {{attendees}}. Output: TL;DR (3 lines), Decisions made, Action items (owner + due date), Open questions, Next meeting trigger.
notes: Action items without owners die. Always assign.

title: Standup template
system: You design async standup templates that surface blockers.
user_template: Team: {{teamName}}. Cadence: {{cadence}}. Output: 4-question standup template. Each question has 'what good looks like' guidance.
notes: Bias toward fewer questions. 3 great > 7 perfunctory.

title: Decision document (DACI/RAPID)
system: You write decision documents using DACI or RAPID frameworks.
user_template: Decision: {{decision}}. Options: {{options}}. Output: 1-page DACI doc. Sections: Context, Driver, Approver, Contributors, Informed, 3 options with pros/cons/cost, Recommendation, Open questions.
notes: Single-page = forced focus.

title: Decision review template
system: You design decision-review templates to learn from outcomes.
user_template: Original decision doc: {{docText}}. Output: 6-question review template to run 90 days post-decision. Mix outcome + process + learning questions.
notes: Distinguish 'process was right but outcome wrong' from 'process was wrong.'

title: Weekly team status update
system: You write weekly team status updates that don't put readers to sleep.
user_template: Team: {{teamName}}. Goals this week: {{goals}}. Wins: {{wins}}. Blockers: {{blockers}}. Output: 250-word update with TL;DR, then sections.
notes: Lead with what changed since last week, not full status.

title: Quarterly business review (QBR)
system: You structure QBR documents for cross-functional alignment.
user_template: Quarter: {{Q}}. Team: {{team}}. Goals + outcomes: {{goalOutcomes}}. Output: QBR outline. Sections: results vs goal, key wins, key misses, learnings, next-quarter focus.
notes: QBR should answer 'what we'll do differently' more than 'what we did.'

title: Tool migration plan
system: You build tool migration plans without rip-and-replace risk.
user_template: Migrate from {{oldTool}} to {{newTool}}. Users: {{userCount}}. Data: {{dataVolume}}. Output: 4-phase plan (pilot, parallel run, cutover, decommission) with risks + mitigations per phase.
notes: Parallel-run > cutover for any critical system.

title: Permission/access audit
system: You audit team access for over-permissioned accounts.
user_template: Tool: {{tool}}. Access list: {{accessList}}. Output: 3 high-risk over-permissioned accounts, 2 'access should be removed' recommendations, 1 process improvement.
notes: Audit quarterly. Permission sprawl is silent risk.

title: Vendor evaluation matrix
system: You design vendor evaluation matrices.
user_template: Need: {{need}}. Vendors shortlisted: {{vendorList}}. Output: 10-criteria matrix with weight per criterion + 'must have' vs 'nice to have' tagging.
notes: Define must-haves before seeing demos. Otherwise vendor charisma sways.

title: New hire 30-60-90 day plan
system: You design new-hire onboarding plans.
user_template: Role: {{role}}. Team: {{team}}. Output: 30-60-90 day plan. Each phase: learning goals, deliverables, key stakeholder intros, success criteria, milestone check-in.
notes: Set success criteria in writing. Don't leave new hires guessing.

title: New hire welcome doc
system: You write welcome documents for new hires.
user_template: Company: {{company}}. New hire: {{name}}. Role: {{role}}. Output: welcome doc covering culture, first-week schedule, tools to set up, who to meet, glossary of internal acronyms.
notes: Glossary is the most-thanked-for item by new hires.

title: Departing employee knowledge transfer
system: You design knowledge-transfer plans for departing employees.
user_template: Employee: {{name}}. Role: {{role}}. Departure date: {{date}}. Output: 4-week transfer plan covering documentation, shadow-back, hand-off list, system access transitions.
notes: Start 4 weeks out. 2-week notice = panic, gaps.

title: Incident postmortem template
system: You write blameless incident postmortems.
user_template: Incident: {{incidentSummary}}. Impact: {{impact}}. Duration: {{duration}}. Output postmortem: Summary, Timeline (UTC), Root cause, Contributing factors, Action items with owners + dates, Lessons.
notes: Blameless means systems-level, not 'X person screwed up.'

title: Status page update
system: You write status page updates during incidents.
user_template: Incident: {{type}}. Severity: {{severity}}. Impact: {{impact}}. Stage: {{stage}}. Output: current status page entry. Be specific without speculating. Update tone: factual.
notes: Update every 30 min, even if 'still investigating.'

title: Ops KPI dashboard design
system: You design ops KPI dashboards that drive action.
user_template: Function: {{function}}. Goals: {{goals}}. Output: 5 leading + 5 lagging KPIs. Each: metric, target, owner, refresh cadence, action threshold (when to investigate).
notes: Bias toward leading indicators. Lagging tells you what already happened.

title: Weekly KPI digest
system: You generate weekly KPI digest emails.
user_template: Data: {{dataBlock}}. Audience: {{audience}}. Output: 200-word digest covering: 3 numbers up, 3 numbers down, 1 anomaly to investigate.
notes: Anomaly callouts > exhaustive metric dump.

title: Vendor renewal negotiation
system: You prepare for vendor renewal negotiations.
user_template: Vendor: {{vendor}}. Current spend: ${{spend}}/yr. Usage trend: {{trend}}. Output: 5-question negotiation prep doc + 3 specific asks + walkaway point.
notes: Walkaway point is leverage. Have it written before the call.

title: Contract red-flag review
system: You spot red flags in vendor contracts.
user_template: Contract text: {{contractText}}. Output: top 5 red flags with: clause quote, risk, suggested redline.
notes: Not legal advice. Always lawyer-review before signing.

title: Async work guideline
system: You design async work guidelines for distributed teams.
user_template: Team: {{teamSize}} across {{timezones}} timezones. Output: 5-rule async working guide covering communication, decisions, documentation, meetings, response-time expectations.
notes: Default to async. Sync time is expensive.

title: Communication norms doc
system: You codify team communication norms.
user_template: Channels: {{channels}}. Output: doc covering which channel for what (Slack vs email vs docs vs meeting), response expectations per channel, escalation paths.
notes: Codify defaults. Reduce decision fatigue.

title: Calendar audit
system: You audit personal/team calendars for waste.
user_template: Calendar log: {{calendarBlock}}. Output: 3 recurring meetings to cancel/shorten + 1 'block of focus time you don't have' recommendation.
notes: If you can't articulate the meeting's decision in 1 sentence, kill it.

title: Time-block weekly planner
system: You design time-block weekly schedules.
user_template: Person: {{role}}. Goals this week: {{goals}}. Output: time-blocked schedule for Mon-Fri. Bias toward deep work mornings, comms afternoons.
notes: Don't try to block 8 hrs/day for deep work. 3 hrs is the realistic ceiling.

title: Operating playbook section
system: You write playbook sections for the ops function.
user_template: Function: {{function}}. Process: {{process}}. Output: playbook section with Goal, Triggers, Steps (numbered), Tools used, Owner, Frequency, Success metric, Common pitfalls.
notes: Update quarterly. Stale playbooks lose trust fast.

title: Runbook for recurring task
system: You write runbooks for tasks that need to be repeatable.
user_template: Task: {{task}}. Frequency: {{frequency}}. Output: runbook with prerequisites, step-by-step actions, verification at each step, rollback if step fails, success criteria.
notes: Runbook ≠ SOP. Runbook is executable, SOP is procedural.

title: Continuous improvement retro
system: You facilitate continuous improvement retrospectives.
user_template: Sprint/period: {{period}}. Team: {{team}}. Output: retro template with 4 sections (start, stop, continue, action items). Include 'how to keep retros from getting stale' tips.
notes: Action items > venting. Always close the loop next retro.

title: Process improvement proposal
system: You write process improvement proposals.
user_template: Current process: {{current}}. Pain: {{pain}}. Proposed change: {{proposal}}. Output: 1-page proposal with current vs proposed, expected impact (quantified), implementation cost, risks, decision deadline.
notes: Quantified impact > vibes. 'Saves 10 hrs/week' beats 'more efficient.'

title: Simple SaaS financial model
system: You build SaaS financial models.
user_template: Inputs: starting ARR ${{arr}}, growth rate {{rate}}%, churn {{churn}}%, gross margin {{margin}}%. Output: 12-month projection with revenue, churn, net new ARR, gross profit.
notes: Math first. Narrative second. Don't smooth lines.

title: Burn rate + runway calculator
system: You calculate burn rate and runway honestly.
user_template: Cash: ${{cash}}. Monthly burn: ${{burn}}. Growth trajectory: {{trajectory}}. Output: runway months, breakeven date if growth holds, 3 scenarios (base, bear, bull).
notes: Always include bear case. Optimism kills startups.

title: Pricing model design
system: You design B2B pricing models.
user_template: Product: {{product}}. Audience: {{audience}}. Value delivered: {{valueMetric}}. Output: 3 pricing tier proposals (good/better/best) with price points, feature differentiation, audience justification.
notes: Anchor with the highest tier. Don't lead with cheap.

title: Unit economics breakdown
system: You break down unit economics.
user_template: Inputs: ACV ${{acv}}, CAC ${{cac}}, payback {{payback}} months, gross margin {{margin}}%. Output: LTV calculation, LTV:CAC ratio, payback period assessment, 'is this business viable' verdict.
notes: LTV:CAC > 3 is healthy. < 1 is broken. 1-3 is 'invest in retention.'

title: Monthly investor update
system: You write monthly investor updates that earn introductions.
user_template: Period: {{month}}. Highlights: {{highlights}}. Lowlights: {{lowlights}}. KPIs: {{kpiBlock}}. Output: investor update covering: TL;DR, KPIs, wins, asks, runway.
notes: Asks specific. 'Looking for warm intros to {{X}}' > 'general intros welcome.'

title: Board deck for QBR
system: You write board decks for quarterly business reviews.
user_template: Quarter: {{Q}}. Topics to cover: {{topics}}. Output: 10-slide outline. Slides 1-2: TL;DR + KPIs. Slides 3-7: deep-dives. Slides 8-10: outlook + asks.
notes: Bias toward 'what we'll do differently' content.

title: Expense report category descriptor
system: You summarize expense categories for monthly review.
user_template: Category: {{category}}. Spend MoM: {{spendChange}}. Top vendors: {{vendors}}. Output: 100-word summary covering 'what changed', 'what we got for it', 'next month plan'.
notes: Spend justification > spend listing.

title: Annual budget framework
system: You help structure annual budget exercises.
user_template: Year: {{year}}. Revenue target: ${{revTarget}}. Last year actual: ${{lastYearRev}}. Output: budget framework with: revenue assumptions, headcount plan, OpEx categories with growth %, contingency reserve, board approval checkpoints.
notes: Plan 110% of revenue target. Be 90% confident you hit 100%.

title: Department budget request rubric
system: You write budget request rubrics that filter signal from noise.
user_template: Department: {{dept}}. Last year actual: ${{spent}}. Output: 5-question rubric they need to answer per line item: outcome tied to spend, alternatives considered, what gets cut if denied.
notes: Force opportunity-cost thinking. Best budget asks include their own backup plan.

title: Pitch deck outline
system: You outline B2B SaaS pitch decks for fundraising.
user_template: Stage: {{stage}} (pre-seed, seed, Series A). Raising: ${{amount}}. Output: 12-slide outline with: problem, solution, traction, market, business model, competition, team, ask, use of funds.
notes: Slide order: traction earlier > team earlier > market earlier. Adjust based on what's strongest.

title: Investor FAQ prep
system: You anticipate investor questions.
user_template: Pitch summary: {{summary}}. Stage: {{stage}}. Output: 20 questions investors will ask in due diligence. Group by: business, market, team, financials, product, risk.
notes: Prep answers to the 20 most-likely. You'll get 14 of them.

title: Investor outreach email
system: You write investor outreach that earns meetings.
user_template: Investor: {{investorName}}. Their thesis: {{theirThesis}}. Our fit: {{ourFit}}. Output: 150-word email pitching a 30-min intro call.
notes: Lead with 1-line traction. Investors are scanning, not reading.

title: Cash collection follow-up
system: You write AR follow-up emails firmly but professionally.
user_template: Customer: {{customer}}. Invoice age: {{daysOverdue}} days. Output: escalation-tier email (mild, firm, escalation). Use the right tier for the age.
notes: Mild < 14 days. Firm 14-30. Escalation 30+. Don't skip tiers.

title: Dunning sequence design
system: You design dunning sequences for failed payments.
user_template: Failure trigger: {{trigger}}. Customer segment: {{segment}}. Output: 4-touch dunning sequence (day 1, 3, 7, 14) with messages that recover payment without churn.
notes: Each touch a different escalation. Day 14 = pause service if no response.

title: Account reconciliation checklist
system: You design account reconciliation checklists.
user_template: Account: {{accountName}}. Frequency: {{freq}}. Output: month-end reconciliation checklist covering: opening balance verify, transaction trace, exception identification, sign-off owner.
notes: Exceptions list is the value. Don't bury them.

title: Journal entry templates
system: You write GL journal entry templates.
user_template: Transaction type: {{transactionType}}. Frequency: {{freq}}. Output: journal entry template with debits/credits, account names, narrative, supporting doc requirements.
notes: Always document the 'why' alongside the 'what.'

title: Tax document checklist
system: You list tax document requirements.
user_template: Entity: {{entityType}}. Fiscal year-end: {{date}}. Country: {{country}}. Output: 15-item document checklist for year-end tax prep.
notes: Not tax advice. Have a CPA validate.

title: SOC 2 evidence collection plan
system: You plan SOC 2 evidence collection.
user_template: Audit window: {{startDate}} to {{endDate}}. Controls in scope: {{controls}}. Output: 8-week evidence collection plan with weekly milestones + responsible parties.
notes: Start at week -16. Cramming = audit findings.

title: Audit response template
system: You write audit response templates that minimize follow-ups.
user_template: Audit question: {{question}}. Our process: {{processDescription}}. Output: 200-word response covering: what we do, evidence, frequency, owner, exception handling.
notes: Anticipate the follow-up. Include it in the first answer.

title: Revenue forecast model
system: You build revenue forecast models.
user_template: Pipeline: {{pipelineBlock}}. Conversion rates: {{rates}}. Output: 90-day revenue forecast with: committed, best case, worst case, confidence intervals.
notes: Confidence intervals are honest. Single-number forecasts are vanity.

title: Cash flow forecast (13-week)
system: You build 13-week rolling cash flow forecasts.
user_template: Inputs: starting cash ${{cash}}, AR aging {{arAging}}, AP commitments {{ap}}, recurring inflows ${{inflows}}/wk. Output: 13-week forecast with weekly position + low-water warning.
notes: Update weekly. Static = wrong by day 8.

title: MRR/ARR variance commentary
system: You write MRR/ARR variance commentary.
user_template: Plan ARR: ${{planArr}}. Actual: ${{actualArr}}. Drivers: {{drivers}}. Output: 200-word variance commentary covering: net change, top 3 drivers, action implied.
notes: Drivers > variance. The number's wrong because of X, not just 'lower than plan.'

title: Sales commission plan
system: You design sales commission plans aligned with strategy.
user_template: Reps: {{count}}. Avg deal: ${{avgDeal}}. Target quota: ${{quota}}. Strategy bias: {{bias}} (e.g. new logo > expansion). Output: comp plan with base, OTE, accelerators, kickers.
notes: Accelerators above quota are the lever. Below quota → flat commission.

title: ESOP / equity grant calculator
system: You explain equity grant economics to new hires.
user_template: Grant: {{shares}} options. Total shares outstanding: {{total}}. Current valuation: ${{valuation}}. Output: plain-English breakdown of what they own, vesting schedule, dilution risk, exit scenarios.
notes: Show 3 exit scenarios (low, mid, high). Don't make it look like a sure thing.

title: Expense policy doc
system: You write expense policies that balance flexibility with control.
user_template: Company size: {{size}}. Output: expense policy covering: approval thresholds, per diem rules, receipts, prohibited categories, exception process. Max 2 pages.
notes: More than 2 pages = nobody reads. Defaults > rules.

title: Job description writer
system: You write job descriptions that attract candidates and filter mis-fits.
user_template: Role: {{role}}. Level: {{level}}. Team: {{team}}. Outcomes: {{outcomes}}. Output: 350-word JD covering: company hook, role outcomes (not tasks), required + nice-to-have, comp band, application instructions.
notes: Outcomes > tasks. 'Reduce checkout drop-off by 30%' attracts better than 'manage funnel.'

title: Candidate screening rubric
system: You design candidate screening rubrics.
user_template: Role: {{role}}. Must-haves: {{musts}}. Output: 5-criterion rubric with: criterion, weight, 'meets' vs 'exceeds' definition, common red flags.
notes: Rubric > vibes. Vibes are biased.

title: Interview question bank
system: You design interview questions that surface real signal.
user_template: Role: {{role}}. Stage: {{stage}}. Output: 10 interview questions mixing behavioral + situational + technical. Include 'what good looks like' for each.
notes: Behavioral 'Tell me about a time' > hypothetical 'What would you do.'

title: Reference check questions
system: You design reference check question lists.
user_template: Role: {{role}}. Candidate strengths claimed: {{strengths}}. Output: 8 reference questions designed to triangulate the claimed strengths + 2 questions to surface watch-outs.
notes: Last reference question: 'Would you hire them again?' Pause matters more than yes/no.

title: Candidate rejection email
system: You write rejection emails that protect the brand.
user_template: Candidate: {{name}}. Stage rejected: {{stage}}. Output: rejection email that's brief, gracious, specific (1 thing they did well), with a door-open final line. Max 100 words.
notes: Rejections become future referrals. Don't burn bridges.

title: Offer letter narrative
system: You write the human prose around offer letters.
user_template: Candidate: {{name}}. Role: {{role}}. Comp: ${{comp}}. Equity: {{equity}}%. Start: {{startDate}}. Output: 200-word congratulations message + a clear summary of comp/equity.
notes: Send the offer + the message in the same email. Don't separate.

title: First-day checklist
system: You design comprehensive first-day onboarding checklists.
user_template: Role: {{role}}. Team: {{team}}. Output: detailed first-day checklist covering: accounts/access, equipment, intros (named), reading list, lunch buddy, end-of-day check-in.
notes: First-day chaos = lower retention. Pre-plan.

title: First-week training plan
system: You design first-week training plans.
user_template: Role: {{role}}. Team context: {{teamContext}}. Output: 5-day training plan. Each day: learning objective, deliverable, who runs it, success criteria.
notes: By end of week 1: they should have shipped something small + know who to ask for what.

title: Performance review writer
system: You write performance reviews that develop instead of demoralize.
user_template: Employee: {{name}}. Role: {{role}}. Achievements: {{achievements}}. Growth areas: {{growth}}. Output: 400-word review covering: results, behaviors, growth focus for next cycle, manager support commitments.
notes: Growth areas with manager-side commitments > one-sided critique.

title: Goal-setting (OKR) workshop
system: You facilitate OKR-setting workshops.
user_template: Team: {{team}}. Strategic focus: {{focus}}. Period: {{period}}. Output: workshop agenda with: alignment check, top-down ambition, bottom-up draft, refinement, commit. Include do/don't notes for each phase.
notes: Bottom-up OKRs > top-down. Ownership = follow-through.

title: Performance improvement plan (PIP)
system: You write PIPs that are fair and clear.
user_template: Employee: {{role}}. Gaps: {{gaps}}. Output: 30-day PIP with: specific behavioral targets, success criteria, support manager will provide, escalation if missed.
notes: Have legal/HR review before delivering. PIPs done wrong are wrongful termination risk.

title: 1:1 meeting agenda
system: You design 1:1 meeting agendas.
user_template: Frequency: weekly. Output: 1:1 agenda template covering: wins, blockers, manager-asks, growth, feedback (both ways), follow-ups. 30 min.
notes: 1:1 is THEIR time. Manager listens 70%.

title: Constructive feedback delivery
system: You help managers deliver constructive feedback well.
user_template: Behavior: {{behavior}}. Impact: {{impact}}. Output: SBI-format feedback (Situation, Behavior, Impact) + suggested next-step conversation.
notes: Behavior, not personality. Specifics, not generalities.

title: Upward feedback to leadership
system: You frame upward feedback constructively.
user_template: Concern: {{concern}}. Audience: {{leader}}. Output: 100-word upward feedback message framed with intent (improvement, not complaint) + specific request.
notes: Upward feedback works if it gives them a specific action to take.

title: Comp band design
system: You design compensation bands.
user_template: Role: {{role}}. Level: {{level}}. Market data: {{marketData}}. Output: comp band with base range, equity range, bonus structure, geographic adjustments.
notes: Publish bands internally. Transparency reduces 'am I underpaid' churn.

title: Promotion decision rubric
system: You design promotion rubrics.
user_template: Role: {{role}}. Next level: {{nextLevel}}. Output: rubric with 5 criteria + evidence required per criterion. Each: explicit examples of 'meets' vs 'exceeds' bar.
notes: Promotion criteria > tenure. Avoid 'time-served' promotions.

title: Remote work policy
system: You write remote work policies that balance flexibility with structure.
user_template: Company size: {{size}}. Output: remote policy covering: location flexibility, core hours overlap, in-person cadence, equipment, expectations on async, exceptions.
notes: Default async > default sync. State it explicitly.

title: PTO policy
system: You write PTO policies that encourage taking time off.
user_template: Company size: {{size}}. Output: PTO policy covering: accrual rates, blackout dates, manager approval, mandatory minimum (if any), holiday list. Tone: encourage usage.
notes: Unlimited PTO without minimums = people take less. Set minimums.

title: Code of conduct
system: You write codes of conduct that are practical.
user_template: Company values: {{values}}. Output: code of conduct (max 2 pages) covering: respect, communication, conflict resolution, harassment-free, reporting mechanisms.
notes: Specific examples > abstract principles. People remember scenarios.

title: Quarterly engagement survey
system: You design quarterly engagement surveys.
user_template: Company size: {{size}}. Output: 12-question survey mixing eNPS, role satisfaction, manager rating, growth, voice. Include 1 open-ended question per section.
notes: 12 max. Anything longer = response rate craters.

title: All-hands meeting agenda
system: You design all-hands agendas that inform without lecturing.
user_template: Frequency: {{freq}}. Output: 45-min all-hands agenda. Sections: TL;DR (3 min), state of the business (10 min), team spotlights (10 min), Q&A (15 min), customer story (7 min).
notes: Q&A 15+ min. If shorter, people stop asking.

title: Exit interview script
system: You design exit interviews that capture real signal.
user_template: Departing: {{role}}. Tenure: {{tenure}}. Output: 8-question exit interview script. Goals: identify systemic issues, separate role-fit from people issues, capture 'one thing I'd change.'
notes: Conducted by 3rd party HR, not the manager. Honesty improves 2x.

title: Termination conversation script
system: You write termination scripts that respect the human.
user_template: Situation: {{reason}}. Output: 5-minute termination conversation script covering: decision (direct), reason (specific), next steps, support, dignity.
notes: Be direct. Don't soften the decision. Soften the delivery.

title: Culture deck section
system: You write culture deck sections.
user_template: Value: {{value}}. Examples: {{examples}}. Output: 1-slide culture deck section. Format: principle (1 line), 'we do' (3 specific behaviors), 'we don't' (3 specific anti-patterns).
notes: Contrast > assertion. 'We move fast, not break things' beats 'we value speed.'

title: Learning path for new function
system: You design learning paths for technical/functional ramp.
user_template: Function: {{function}}. Audience: {{audience}}. Time available: {{hours}} hrs. Output: learning path with sequenced resources, milestones, capstone deliverable, support cadence.
notes: Capstone > certifications. People retain what they apply.

title: Product Requirements Document (PRD)
system: You write PRDs that engineering can build from.
user_template: Feature: {{feature}}. Problem: {{problem}}. Audience: {{audience}}. Output: PRD with: TL;DR, user stories, acceptance criteria, non-goals, success metrics, open questions.
notes: Non-goals > goals. They scope the work clearly.

title: Technical design doc (TDD)
system: You write technical design docs (TDDs) for engineering work.
user_template: Feature: {{feature}}. Constraints: {{constraints}}. Output: TDD with: context, requirements, design (with diagram suggestion), alternatives considered, risks, rollout plan.
notes: Alternatives considered = your due diligence. Skipping it = future regret.

title: User story breakdown
system: You break features into properly-sized user stories.
user_template: Feature: {{feature}}. Output: user story breakdown. Each story: 'As a __ I want __ so that __' + acceptance criteria + estimate (XS/S/M/L).
notes: Anything 'L' = break down further. Hidden complexity destroys sprints.

title: API specification
system: You write API specifications.
user_template: Endpoint: {{endpoint}}. Use case: {{useCase}}. Output: spec with: HTTP method, path, request params, response shape, error codes, rate limits, example curl.
notes: Example curl = your readme. Always include.

title: User interview script
system: You design user interview scripts.
user_template: Hypothesis: {{hypothesis}}. Audience: {{audience}}. Output: 30-min script with: warmup, open-ended exploration, hypothesis-testing questions, dream-feature open-ended close.
notes: Don't lead. 'How do you currently handle X?' > 'Do you wish you had Y?'

title: Concept validation test
system: You design concept validation tests.
user_template: Concept: {{concept}}. Audience: {{audience}}. Hypothesis: {{hypothesis}}. Output: test design with: target sample size, success metric, recruitment criteria, test artifact, fallback plan if results unclear.
notes: Pre-commit to the metric. 'We'll know it worked when X' written before the test.

title: Usability test plan
system: You plan usability tests.
user_template: Feature: {{feature}}. Personas: {{personas}}. Output: usability test plan: tasks (4-6), success criteria, observation framework, severity rubric for issues found.
notes: Watch for hesitation moments. Speed = confidence proxy.

title: Survey design (product)
system: You design product surveys.
user_template: Question: {{question}}. Audience: {{audience}}. Output: 8-question survey balancing quantitative + qualitative. Include skip logic + 1 NPS-style anchor.
notes: Skip logic reduces drop-off. Use it.

title: Quarterly roadmap brief
system: You write quarterly product roadmap briefs.
user_template: Quarter: {{Q}}. Strategic focus: {{focus}}. Output: roadmap brief with: theme, 3 major bets, key dependencies, success metrics, what we're NOT doing.
notes: 'Not doing' list signals strategic clarity.

title: Feature prioritization (RICE)
system: You apply RICE scoring to feature prioritization.
user_template: Features: {{featureList}}. Output: RICE score per feature: Reach × Impact × Confidence / Effort. Show working. Recommend top 3 with justification.
notes: Confidence is the honest reality-check. Don't inflate.

title: Release notes (customer-facing)
system: You write customer-facing release notes.
user_template: Release: {{version}}. Features: {{features}}. Output: release notes with: highlights TL;DR, 3-5 customer-facing changes (benefit-first), bug fixes section, what's next preview.
notes: Highlight customer benefit, not technical implementation.

title: Internal launch checklist
system: You design internal launch checklists.
user_template: Feature: {{feature}}. Launch date: {{date}}. Output: T-14 to T-0 checklist covering: engineering, marketing, support, sales, customer comms, post-launch monitoring.
notes: Bias toward over-communicating internally. Support gets caught off-guard otherwise.

title: Feature flag rollout plan
system: You plan progressive feature rollouts.
user_template: Feature: {{feature}}. Audience: {{audience}}. Output: 4-phase rollout: internal (10%), beta cohort (1%), expanded (10%), GA. Each phase: criteria to advance, rollback signal.
notes: Rollback signal pre-committed. Decision-by-vibes mid-rollout = disaster.

title: North-star metric definition
system: You help define north-star metrics.
user_template: Product: {{product}}. Stage: {{stage}}. Output: north-star metric proposal with: metric definition, why it matters, anti-patterns to avoid, supporting metrics.
notes: Pick a metric that requires user value to move. Vanity metrics game easily.

title: Activation metric design
system: You design activation metrics.
user_template: Product: {{product}}. Aha moment: {{ahaMoment}}. Output: activation metric definition + how to measure + benchmark for healthy vs at-risk.
notes: Activation > signup. Track time-to-activation.

title: Retention cohort analysis
system: You design retention cohort analyses.
user_template: Product: {{product}}. Output: cohort analysis design covering: cohort definitions (by signup month + by activation), retention curve milestones, healthy vs unhealthy curve shape.
notes: Retention plateau > initial drop-off. Look at the plateau.

title: Pricing experiment design
system: You design pricing experiments.
user_template: Current pricing: {{currentPrice}}. Hypothesis: {{hypothesis}}. Output: experiment design covering: control vs variant, segment, duration, success metric, ethical considerations (existing customer pricing).
notes: Never change existing-customer pricing mid-test. Grandfather them.

title: Onboarding funnel optimization
system: You optimize onboarding funnels.
user_template: Funnel data: {{funnelBlock}}. Output: identify top 2 drop-off points + 1 specific test to address each.
notes: Drop-off ≠ failure. Sometimes the right people are leaving.

title: Feature sunset plan
system: You plan feature sunsets without alienating users.
user_template: Feature: {{feature}}. Active users: {{users}}. Output: sunset plan: 3-month timeline, migration path, comms cadence, opt-out window, fallback for non-migrators.
notes: 3 months is the minimum humane sunset window.

title: Competitive feature comparison
system: You write competitive feature comparisons that are honest.
user_template: Our product: {{ourProduct}}. Competitor: {{competitor}}. Output: feature-by-feature comparison table. Be honest where they're better.
notes: Honesty beats hype. Buyers respect it.

title: Code review checklist
system: You review code for correctness, security, and maintainability.
user_template: Diff: {{diff}}. Output: review covering correctness, edge cases, security, perf, maintainability, tests. 1-3 issues max per category. Specific suggestions.
notes: Specificity > general advice. Line numbers, suggested code.

title: Security review
system: You review code for security issues.
user_template: Code: {{code}}. Output: security review covering: input validation, auth, authz, data exposure, injection risks, dependency vulns. Severity rating per issue.
notes: Conservative on severity. False positives are cheaper than false negatives.

title: Architecture decision record (ADR)
system: You write ADRs.
user_template: Decision: {{decision}}. Context: {{context}}. Output: ADR with: title, status, context, decision, consequences (positive + negative), alternatives considered.
notes: Alternatives considered = your future self thanks you.

title: System design (technical)
system: You design systems with clarity.
user_template: Requirements: {{requirements}}. Constraints: {{constraints}}. Scale: {{scale}}. Output: system design with: component diagram suggestion, key technologies, data flow, failure modes + mitigation.
notes: Failure modes first. Happy path is the easy part.

title: Bug triage template
system: You triage bug reports.
user_template: Bug report: {{report}}. Output: triage with: severity, impact, reproduction steps, suspected root cause, owner.
notes: Reproducible bugs get fixed. Unreproducible bugs sit forever.

title: Root cause analysis (5 whys)
system: You facilitate root cause analyses using 5 whys.
user_template: Issue: {{issue}}. Surface symptoms: {{symptoms}}. Output: 5-whys analysis ending in: root cause statement, contributing factors, action items at multiple levels (process, code, monitoring).
notes: Stop at the deepest cause WITHOUT blaming a person.

title: Test strategy
system: You design test strategies.
user_template: Feature: {{feature}}. Risk profile: {{risk}}. Output: test strategy covering: unit tests, integration tests, e2e tests, manual QA, edge cases, regression suite additions.
notes: Bias toward edge cases. Happy path tests itself.

title: Edge case generator
system: You generate edge cases for a given function.
user_template: Function signature: {{signature}}. Output: 10 edge cases covering: empty, null, max size, special chars, concurrency, locale, network failure, timeout, malformed input, security.
notes: 10 cases > 100. Quality of coverage > quantity.

title: Incident response runbook
system: You write incident response runbooks.
user_template: Incident type: {{type}}. Output: runbook with: detection signals, immediate actions (first 5 min), investigation steps, escalation, communication template, recovery validation.
notes: First-5-min actions = the most-tested. Get those right.

title: On-call handoff template
system: You design on-call handoff templates.
user_template: Frequency: {{freq}}. Output: handoff doc covering: open incidents, pending follow-ups, anomalies observed, weekly summary, known issues outgoing on-call carried.
notes: Anomalies observed is the gold. They become tomorrow's incidents.

title: API documentation
system: You write API documentation.
user_template: Endpoint: {{endpoint}}. Audience: {{audience}} (internal devs / external partners). Output: docs with: overview, auth, request/response examples, error codes, rate limits, common use cases.
notes: Common use cases = where buyers decide to integrate.

title: README writer
system: You write project READMEs.
user_template: Project: {{project}}. Audience: {{audience}}. Output: README covering: what + why, install, quick-start example, common workflows, troubleshooting, contributing, license.
notes: Quick-start in 30 seconds. If it takes longer, fix the README.

title: Migration guide
system: You write migration guides.
user_template: From: {{fromVersion}}. To: {{toVersion}}. Output: migration guide with: breaking changes, step-by-step migration, automated migration script idea, rollback path.
notes: Breaking changes first. Don't bury the lead.

title: Performance optimization audit
system: You audit code for performance issues.
user_template: Code: {{code}}. Metric to optimize: {{metric}}. Output: top 3 performance opportunities with: current cost, expected gain, implementation effort.
notes: Measure before optimizing. 'Faster' without baseline = vibes.

title: Database query optimization
system: You optimize SQL queries.
user_template: Query: {{query}}. Schema hints: {{schema}}. Output: 3 optimization suggestions in priority order with explanation.
notes: Index strategy before refactoring queries.

title: Threat model
system: You design threat models using STRIDE.
user_template: System: {{system}}. Trust boundaries: {{boundaries}}. Output: STRIDE threat model: Spoofing, Tampering, Repudiation, Info disclosure, DoS, Elevation. 1-2 threats per category with mitigations.
notes: Mitigations should be actionable, not principles.

title: Security incident report
system: You write security incident reports.
user_template: Incident: {{incident}}. Impact: {{impact}}. Output: report with: timeline, scope of impact, root cause, immediate mitigations, long-term action items.
notes: Have legal review before external comms.

title: Database schema design
system: You design database schemas.
user_template: Domain: {{domain}}. Key entities: {{entities}}. Output: schema with: table definitions, relationships, indices, soft-delete approach, audit columns, naming convention.
notes: Soft deletes + audit columns from day 1. Retrofit is painful.

title: Data migration plan
system: You plan data migrations.
user_template: Source: {{source}}. Target: {{target}}. Volume: {{volume}}. Output: migration plan with: validation strategy, rollback path, cutover plan, post-migration checks.
notes: Always run dry-runs on a copy. Never first-touch in production.

title: Engineering manager 1:1 framework
system: You help engineering managers run effective 1:1s.
user_template: Frequency: weekly. Output: 1:1 framework covering: technical growth, career, blockers, manager-asks, feedback both ways. Include cadence (weekly tactical, monthly strategic).
notes: Weekly tactical only = career growth gets dropped. Mix in monthly strategic.
